capabilityClaimProvenance.unsupportedClaimPolicy: Any framework comparison or production-readiness claim without local evidence, a gate command, a risk class, and an adopter action must be downgraded to report-only or removed before release.

targetDocs.unsupportedClaimHandling: README capability and framework comparison language must reference claim-provenance ids or be downgraded before release.
Documentation home claims must stay tied to runnable adopter evidence and release gates.
Migration decision claims must reference provenance ids and retain rollback gate language.
Framework gap claims must stay aligned with the capability claim provenance contract.

claims.claim: HTTP binding, validation, OpenAPI schema, and error envelope behavior are governed by local runtime and schema evidence.
claims.adopterAction: treat generated REST behavior as stable only after invalid request smoke and OpenAPI/runtime binding checks pass
claims.unsupportedClaimHandling: downgrade HTTP DX claims to candidate when the schema or invalid request smoke evidence is missing

claims.claim: Generated project upgrades are repeatable and have explainable diff, dependency, smoke, and rollback evidence.
claims.adopterAction: regenerate into a temporary project, inspect classified diffs, and pin the previous generator profile on failure
claims.unsupportedClaimHandling: remove scaffold compatibility claims when repeat generation, compile smoke, or rollback evidence is absent

claims.claim: RPC, gRPC, and Kitex coexistence boundaries are tracked with benchmark, resolver, balancer, stream, and rollback evidence.
claims.adopterAction: use RPC surfaces behind the listed boundary checks and keep rollback notes for Kitex coexistence migrations
claims.unsupportedClaimHandling: keep RPC Tier 1 language out of release notes until stream, resolver, balancer, and benchmark evidence pass

claims.claim: Production readiness claims are limited to reference-app, runtime SLO, incident drill, and release evidence surfaces.
claims.adopterAction: treat production proof as local reference evidence and rerun the smoke plus runtime SLO gates before adoption
claims.unsupportedClaimHandling: downgrade production-ready wording to reference-only when topology smoke or incident rehearsal evidence is missing

claims.claim: Release trust is based on readiness score, evidence consumption, required checks, security gates, and artifact manifests.
claims.adopterAction: promote releases only when readiness score, evidence consumption, required checks, and security gates are complete
claims.unsupportedClaimHandling: block release-trust claims when a required artifact, check, or security gate is missing

claims.claim: Plugin ecosystem claims are limited to manifest, digest, permission, compatibility, and failure-isolation evidence.
claims.adopterAction: accept third-party plugins only after conformance, permission review, digest verification, and compatibility checks pass
claims.unsupportedClaimHandling: remove publishable-plugin wording when conformance evidence or failure isolation is incomplete

claims.claim: Performance comparison claims are evidence-scoped to benchmark trends and ratcheted budgets, with latency report-only unless promoted.
claims.adopterAction: use benchmark rows as trend evidence and block only the allocation budgets promoted by the ratchet policy
claims.unsupportedClaimHandling: mark performance claims as report-only unless trend evidence, budget policy, and regression gates are present

dimensions.gap: Mature frameworks make adopter-facing claims easy to trace to runnable evidence. gofly now has many evidence contracts, but lacks one traceability map linking claims, source manifests, report fields, gate commands, and rollback or escalation actions.
dimensions.todo: Add an evidence traceability contract and gate it through governance report checks so top-level claims cannot drift away from source evidence.

dimensions.gap: Top frameworks reduce upgrade risk with predictable migration rehearsal paths. gofly has generated dry-run and dependency evidence, but needs one adoption rehearsal contract tying regenerate, dependency, release evidence, smoke, and rollback steps together.
dimensions.todo: Add an upgrade rehearsal contract that maps each rehearsal step to evidence, gate commands, expected output, failure class, and rollback path.

dimensions.gap: Production frameworks are trusted faster when operational failure drills are concrete. gofly has runtime SLO and operator drills, but should expose incident rehearsal rows with symptoms, artifacts, rollback triggers, and post-incident evidence.
dimensions.todo: Extend runtime SLO governance with incident drill evidence covering diagnosis, mitigation, rollback trigger, and post-incident artifact requirements.

dimensions.gap: Framework comparison and production-readiness claims become risky when they outpace local proof. gofly has governed evidence, but needs claim provenance to prevent unsupported claims in docs and reports.
dimensions.todo: Add a capability claim provenance contract that requires every comparison or production-readiness claim to name source evidence, gate, risk class, adopter action, and unsupported-claim handling.
